Teachable Video Shows Blank? Browser Strict Privacy Settings Are the Likely Cause

Some readers told me Teachable videos suddenly stopped playing, but my own browser was fine. Turned out the culprit was their browser's Strict privacy mode. This post walks through the fix in Firefox, Chrome, and Edge, plus the fallback when toggling privacy mode isn't enough.
Learn React #19 — Spinning Up a Project with Create React App (CRA)
You'll Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now?

This kicks off a brand new chapter — the Create React App (CRA) chapter. From here on we'll slowly move away from CodePen and start working in the kind of development environment you'd actually use for real projects. I'll walk through what CRA is, how to spin up a project, and what the generated folder structure looks like.
How to Add ESLint to a Vue Vite Project
This post walks through adding ESLint to a Vue Vite project, using the Airbnb or Standard style guide.
React Fragments: How to Stop Wrapping Everything in a Pointless div - (18)

Fragments are one of those React tricks you'll reach for constantly in real projects. They let you avoid wrapping your JSX in an extra `div`, which keeps your HTML structure clean. So how do they work, and what's the difference between `React.Fragment` and the empty `<></>` shorthand? Let's take a look.
Learn React Sooner or Later, So Why Not Now? - React Advanced - Custom Hooks - (17)
Learn React Sooner or Later, So Why Not Now?

We've met a bunch of React Hooks in the earlier chapters, but every one of them was provided by React itself. So what if you want to build your own Hook? In this post we'll get to know Custom Hooks — what they actually are, the one rule you have to follow, and a couple of small examples (useKeyDown and useLog) to make it click.
You'll Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now? - React Advanced - useRef - (16)

Next up is a pretty fun Hook called useRef. What's so special about it? In short, it doesn't trigger a re-render, it hands you back an object with a current property, and it's great for holding values that shouldn't change — or for grabbing a DOM node. Let's take a look.
React Day 15: useReducer — useState's Grown-Up Cousin
You're Gonna Learn React Eventually, Why Not Now?

Next up is something kind of special: useReducer. You can think of it as a beefed-up version of useState. When your state starts getting complicated, useReducer gives you a much cleaner way to manage it, instead of juggling a single variable like you do with useState. We'll walk through the syntax, build a counter, and even fake a mini-Redux with useReducer + useContext.
Vite Deployed to GitHub Pages but _plugin-vue_export-helper Returns 404

Vite builds and ships to GitHub Pages fine — but once you load the page, the dynamically imported _plugin-vue_export-helper module 404s and the page goes blank. This post walks through the actual cause (Rollup's filename sanitization producing a name GitHub Pages doesn't serve) and the vite.config.js tweak that fixes it.
React Day 14 — useCallback, or How I Stopped an Infinite setTimeout Loop

This one's a short and easy chapter. We're just going to quickly get to know useCallback. So what is useCallback? It's pretty much a variation of useMemo — but instead of memoizing a value, it memoizes a function. We'll look at a fun little example where a component falls into an infinite re-render loop, and how useCallback gets us out of it.
React Day 13: useMemo, Basically React's Version of Vue Computed

Starting this chapter we're going to meet a new Hook: useMemo. But before we get into useMemo itself, let's take a quick detour back to Vue and look at something pretty similar — Vue's Computed. Once you've seen how Computed only recalculates when its data changes, useMemo will feel a lot more familiar.
